summary: Encode --html / --monospace / --encrypted as integer 1 in messages_send body, matching Pushover's binary-as-integer convention.
reason: Pushover's /1/messages.json takes binary on/off flags as integer 1, not JSON true. The novel notify command already did this correctly (body["html"] = 1), but the direct messages_send CLI bound the cobra BoolVars and assigned `body["html"] = bodyHtml` — so json.Marshal emitted "html":true. HTML / monospace formatting was silently dropped; --encrypted was the worst case because it would have made Pushover treat an encrypted payload as plaintext on the device. All three (Greptile flagged html / monospace; encrypted has the same line shape and same defect class) now encode as integer 1, with the original CLI BoolVar shape preserved so the user experience stays --html instead of forcing --html=1.
validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cli -run TestMessagesSendBoolFlagsAsIntegerOne -count=1 stands up an httptest server, drives `messages send --html --monospace --encrypted`, and asserts the captured body decodes each of the three keys as a JSON number 1 (not a JSON bool).
